commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

generation.compiledCode: import{jsx as a,jsxs as r}from"react/jsx-runtime";import{Card as f,Stack as i,Divider as s,Text as o,Heading as S,Box as v}from"@ggui-ai/design/primitives";function y(n){const{city:t="Unknown City",temperature:c=0,condition:g="Clear",humidity:d=0,windSpeed:l=0,unit:u="C",forecast:p=[]}=n;return a(f,{shadow:"md",padding:24,radius:"lg",border:!0,children:r(i,{gap:16,children:[r(i,{gap:4,children:[a(S,{level:2,color:"var(--ggui-color-onSurface)",children:t}),a(o,{variant:"label",color:"var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant)",children:g})]}),r(i,{direction:"horizontal",justify:"between",align:"center",children:[r(o,{size:"4xl",weight:"bold",color:"var(--ggui-color-primary-600)",children:[c,"\xB0",u]}),r(i,{gap:4,align:"end",children:[r(o,{variant:"bodySmall",color:"var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant)",children:["Humidity: ",d,"%"]}),r(o,{variant:"bodySmall",color:"var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant)",children:["Wind: ",l," km/h"]})]})]}),a(s,{margin:8}),a(i,{direction:"horizontal",justify:"between",align:"center",children:p.map((e,m)=>a(v,{paddingX:8,paddingY:8,borderRadius:"var(--ggui-shape-radius-md, 8px)",background:"var(--ggui-color-surfaceVariant, #f4f4f5)",children:r(i,{align:"center",gap:4,children:[a(o,{variant:"overline",color:"var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant)",children:e.day??"-"}),a(o,{size:"xl",children:e.icon??"\u2601\uFE0F"}),r(o,{variant:"bodySmall",color:"var(--ggui-color-onSurface)",children:[e.high,"\xB0/",e.low,"\xB0"]})]})},m))})]})})}export{y as default};

issues.description: The weather forecast items are purely static and lack hover or interactive feedback states.
issues.fix: Add a subtle hover background color transition to the forecast boxes to imply interactivity if they were to lead to detailed views.

issues.description: The spacing between the individual forecast items in the horizontal strip is slightly tight, potentially causing overcrowding on smaller screens.
issues.fix: Increase the gap property in the forecast horizontal Stack or wrap the component if it exceeds container width.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and uses the design system primitives effectively to display all requested weather data. While it is functionally complete and visually clean, adding subtle interactive states to the forecast tiles would enhance the user experience and perceived polish.

evaluation.critique: The component has good structure and hierarchy with proper use of ggui primitives, but has inconsistent design token usage—mixing semantic tokens (onSurface) with non-existent color names (primary-600) and hardcoding fallback values instead of relying purely on design system variables. The forecast items lack responsive wrapping and would overflow on small screens.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Forecast items have hover states but are not keyboard-navigable or marked as interactive.
issues.fix: Change the forecast wrapper divs to button or focusable div elements with appropriate ARIA roles if they are intended to be interactive.

issues.description: The main weather icon logic is a simple string match which might fail for variations like 'Partly Sunny'.
issues.fix: Pass the icon itself through the forecast prop or use a more robust mapping object for conditions.

evaluations.critique: The component is excellently structured with a very high attention to visual hierarchy and design system compliance. The use of transitions and state-driven hover effects gives it a premium feel, though accessibility for the forecast sub-elements could be improved.

evaluation.critique: The component has good overall structure and data handling, but suffers from inconsistent design token usage—mixing proper CSS variables with hardcoded hex colors and inline styles with raw pixel values (e.g., padding={16}, margin={8}). The layout is responsive and well-organized, though some inline style properties bypass the design system for transitions and borders.
